behavior_summary: This script implements a one-time activation guard for a firebomb item: when the item is hit, it checks an internal quantity/flag (Item::getQ) and, if allowed, marks the item as activated/consumed (Item::setQ(1)) and may spawn a follow-up behavior (spawn METHOD::0676) that likely represents the bomb's explosion/effect.

Both U8P_FIREBOMB::FIREBOMB::gotHit and U8P_FIREBOMB::FIREBOMB::hit implement the same guard-and-trigger pattern and use the attacker parameter to decide whether to run the spawn: they query Item::isNpc(attacker) and Npc::isDead(attacker) as part of the decision.
The spawn's return value is captured into a temporary local but otherwise ignored; damage parameter is present in the signature but not used by the visible logic.
notable_control_flow: Entry on a hit event leads to an early guard check via Item::getQ; if the guard blocks the activation the function returns without side effects.
If the guard allows activation, Item::setQ(1) is called immediately to prevent further activations (one-time use semantics).

Attacker-related checks (Item::isNpc and Npc::isDead) determine whether the spawn of METHOD::0676 occurs; exact boolean combination is inferred but the net effect is that only certain attacker states permit triggering.
Spawn of METHOD::0676 is invoked with a byte flag 0x01 and the firebomb item reference; execution continues after storing the spawn return value into a temp.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the Q field (Item::Q): is non-zero meaning "armed" or "already triggered"? The code sets Q=1 to mark state, so the intended initial values and transitions are inferred.
Precise attacker conditions that permit the spawn: control-flow shows tests for 'isNpc' and 'isDead' but the exact logical combination (AND/OR/negation) is ambiguous from disassembly.
What METHOD::0676 precisely does: likely explosion/effect animation and damage, but its exact gameplay effects are not present in this script.
Whether hit and U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit are invoked from different engine contexts (e.g., melee vs scripted damage) or are duplicates wired to the same event pipeline.

main_function.name: U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle the firebomb item being struck: guard against repeated activation using the item's Q field and, if conditions about the attacker are satisfied, spawn a follow-up method (likely the explosion/effect) and mark the item as consumed.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine on Event 6 'U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit' when the item is struck.
Only proceeds past the guard when Item::getQ indicates the item is in the activatable state (inferred); otherwise returns early.
Requires an attacker parameter (uword) to evaluate attacker-related conditions (Item::isNpc / Npc::isDead).

function_map.name: U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit
function_map.role: Primary hit handler that enforces one-time activation guard and conditionally spawns the firebomb effect.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREBOMB::hit
function_map.role: Alternate/duplicate hit handler mirroring U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit behavior (same guard-and-trigger semantics).

function_map.name: Item::getQ
function_map.role: Read the item's quantity/flag field used here as the one-time activation guard.

function_map.name: Item::setQ
function_map.role: Write the item's quantity/flag field (set to 1) to mark activation/consumption.

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Test whether the attacker reference is an NPC; used to decide spawn eligibility.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Test whether the attacker NPC is dead; used as an alternate condition that can permit spawning.

function_map.name: spawn METHOD::0676
function_map.role: Spawn/dispatch a method (called with byte flag 0x01 and the firebomb item) that likely performs the explosion/effect.

external_calls.purpose: Check the item's Q/quantity field to decide whether the firebomb can activate (guard against repeated use).
Set the item's Q to 1 to mark it as activated/consumed and prevent further triggers.
Determine whether the attacker parameter refers to an NPC as part of the conditional logic that decides whether to spawn METHOD::0676.
Check whether the attacker NPC is dead; used as an additional/alternative condition to permit spawning the effect.
Trigger the firebomb's explosion/effect behavior with a flag (0x01) and the firebomb item as arguments; the return value is stored but unused.
Same as U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit: guard check for one-time activation.
Same as U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit: mark item as activated/consumed.
Same as U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit: attacker NPC test for spawn eligibility.
Same as U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit: attacker-dead test used in condition.
Same as U8P_FIREBOMB::gotHit: trigger the explosion/effect.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a one-time activation guard for a firebomb item: when the item is hit, it checks an internal quantity/flag (Item::getQ) and, if allowed, marks the item as activated/consumed (Item::setQ(1)) and may spawn a follow-up behavior (spawn METHOD::0676) that likely represents the bomb's explosion/effect.
